rulings.Case Title: NAOMI YAMAMOTO VS. UNIONBANCAL CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 14, 2011, Line 5. DEFENDANTS UNIONBANCAL CORPORATION AND UNION BANK N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendants¿ Motion for Summary Judgment is denied and their alternative Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to the second, third and fourth causes of action in the First Amended Complaint. For the first and fifth causes of action, there are triable issues of fact whether Plaintiff was misclassified as exempt. (Lab. Code § 515(a); see {Plaintiff¿s Response to Separate. Statement Nos. 3 ¿ 32, 39.) For the second cause of action, there are no triable issues of fact that Plaintiff received meal breaks and rest periods while classified as exempt. (Lab. Code §§ 226.7 and 512). Furthermore, Plaintiff stipulates that there were no injuries when she was reclassified as non-exempt. For the third cause of action, there are no triable issues of fact that no injuries resulted from the alleged violation of Labor Code section 226(a). For the fourth cause of action, there are no triable issues of fact that Defendants did not willfully violate Labor Code section 203. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RUSSELL BRIMER VS. PROVO CRAFT & NOVELTY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Req. For Production Of Documents From Deft Provo Craft & Novelty, Inc.; Req. For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 14, 2011, Line 6. PLAINTIFF RUSSELL BRIMER'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents From Defendant Provo Craft & Novelty, Inc., and Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff¿s Motion to compel further discovery responses is granted in part and denied in part. Further responses are required as to the specific Cuttlebug binder that is the subject of the certificate of merit and the toxin lead, but not as to other products or toxins. Investigative activities undertaken at the direction of counsel need not be disclosed. To the extent that the foregoing does not resolve all the disputes at issue in this motion, the parties are required to meet and confer in an effort to resolve or narrow those disputes and report on their progress in doing so at the hearing. All requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: YOSEMITE PROPERTIES VS. MAYTA & JENSEN INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 14, 2011, Line 19. DEFENDANT MAYTA & JENSEN INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Mayta & Jensen Inc.¿s Demurrer to Complaint is sustained without leave to amend. The complaint and attachments demonstrate that plaintiff served a nonpayment notice claiming the August rent before that rent was delinquent. The rent estimate was not reasonable as a matter of law. Delta Imports does not bar demurrers in unlawful detainers. See CCP 1170. The court further notes that plaintiff failed to comply with the fictitious business name statutes. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)
